questions.question: The `--endian=` option reorder input bytes, to handle inputs with differing byte orders, or to provide consistent output independent of the endian convention of the current system.
questions.explanation: Correct. `--endian=` option reorder input bytes, to handle inputs with differing byte orders, or to provide consistent output independent of the endian convention of the current system.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `-v` option dump n input bytes per output line.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. `-v` option produces consecutive lines that are identical.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `-x` option recognize the non-option label argument that traditional od accepted.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. `-x` option produces as hexadecimal two-byte units.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `-l` option outputs as octal two-byte units.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. `-l` option produces as decimal long ints.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `-N` option outputs at most bytes bytes of the input.
